fundraising_information.nature_of_the_event_or_fundraiser:
Community “Paws in the Park” Dog Walk & Adoption Awareness Day at Royer Park in Roseville. The event will be a family-friendly, outdoor fundraising walk where participants register themselves and their dogs, collect pledges from friends and family, and then complete a 2-mile loop through the park. In addition to the walk, we will host:

- A small vendor fair featuring local pet-related businesses (groomers, trainers, pet bakeries, pet photographers, etc.) who will each donate a portion of their day-of sales.
- A low-cost microchip and vaccination clinic operated by a licensed local veterinarian and vet tech volunteers.
- A Placer SPCA information and adoption awareness booth with adoptable animal profiles, volunteer information, and foster program sign-ups (no on-site adoptions will be finalized that day, but meet-and-greet information will be shared).
- A kids’ activity zone with face painting, a coloring station focused on pet care education, and a “build your own pet toy” craft table using donated materials.
- A short educational program on responsible pet ownership, the importance of spay/neuter, and what Placer SPCA does in the community, including a short talk from a recent adopter.

The event is designed to be casual, inclusive, and welcoming to all ages and dog breeds (with clear rules about leashes, vaccinations, and behavior). We will charge a modest registration fee per adult participant, offer a discounted family package, and encourage participants to raise additional funds via pledges. All proceeds, after minimal event expenses, will be donated to Placer SPCA to support medical care, spay/neuter programs, and shelter enrichment.
fundraising_information.how_the_event_or_fundraiser_will_be_adve:
We will use a combination of digital, print, and word-of-mouth promotion to reach a broad audience in Placer County and surrounding areas:

1. Social Media:
- Create a dedicated Facebook event page hosted by our organizing team and invite local community groups (e.g., Roseville community groups, dog-owner groups, local mom groups).
- Post weekly countdown updates on Facebook and Instagram, including photos of our own rescue pets, sponsor spotlights, and short educational blurbs about Placer SPCA’s impact (e.g., success stories, number of animals helped last year).
- Use Instagram Stories and Reels to highlight event preparations (assembling goodie bags, walking the route, meeting with vendors) and tag Placer SPCA and participating businesses.
- Encourage vendors and sponsors to share the event on their own social channels and to cross-promote using a common hashtag: #PawsInTheParkRoseville.

2. Email and Newsletters:
- Send an email invitation and reminder series to my personal and professional contacts (I work as a project coordinator at a local engineering firm and have permission to share with our internal community outreach list).
- Provide a short event blurb and flyer image to local neighborhood associations and PTA groups to include in their email newsletters.

3. Flyers and Posters:
- Print color flyers (8.5" x 11") and distribute them at local coffee shops, pet supply stores, vet clinics, dog groomers, and community centers (e.g., Maidu Community Center, Roseville Sports Center).
- Ask participating vendors to display a flyer at their place of business.

4. Local Media and Community Calendars:
- Submit the event to local online community calendars (Roseville Today, City of Roseville events calendar, local Patch site).
- Send a short press release to the Roseville Press Tribune and local radio stations (e.g., KNCI, The Eagle) to see if they can include a mention in their community events segments.

5. Direct Outreach:
- Personally invite colleagues, neighbors, and extended family via email and text, providing the registration link and emphasizing that dogs are welcome but not required.
- Ask a few local dog trainers we know to share the event with their client lists and at their group classes.

All promotional materials will clearly state that this is a third-party fundraiser with net proceeds benefiting Placer SPCA and will include the Placer SPCA name and logo only in ways that comply with their branding guidelines.
fundraising_information.how_participants_or_donors_will_receive_:
We will use a combination of email acknowledgments and optional mailed thank-you notes, depending on the donor’s preference and the size of the gift, to ensure everyone is properly thanked and has documentation for their records:

1. Registration and Pledge Tracking System:
- We will use an online registration and pledge platform (likely Eventbrite combined with a simple Google Form or an online pledge-tracking spreadsheet) where participants enter their name, email address, and mailing address when they sign up.
- For donors who give directly through the online platform, the system will automatically capture their email and donation amount.

2. Email Thank-You Letters:
- Within one week after the event, I will export a list of all donors and participants with their email addresses and donation amounts.
- I will prepare a personalized email template that includes: the donor’s name, the amount donated (or raised, for participants), the date of the event, a brief summary of how the funds will support Placer SPCA, and a note that Placer SPCA is a 501(c)(3) with the federal tax ID #94-2607682.
- For individual donors giving under $250, this email will serve as their primary acknowledgment and documentation, clearly stating whether any goods or services were received in exchange for their donation (in most cases, no goods or services will be provided beyond the participation experience).

3. Mailed Thank-You Cards for Larger Donors and Sponsors:
- For business sponsors and any individual donors who contribute $250 or more, I will also send a handwritten thank-you card by mail. These cards will:
  - Reference the specific level of sponsorship or donation amount (e.g., “Silver Paw Sponsor – $500”).
  - Reiterate that their contribution is being forwarded to Placer SPCA and how it will make a difference (e.g., approximate number of animals whose vaccinations could be covered).
  - Include my contact information for any questions.

4. Coordination with Placer SPCA (if desired by Placer SPCA):
- When we deliver the funds and final report to Placer SPCA, I will also provide a complete donor list (names, email addresses, mailing addresses, and amounts) so that Placer SPCA has the option to send their own official tax acknowledgment letters directly from the organization.
- If Placer SPCA prefers to handle donor acknowledgments entirely, we can adjust the plan so that our emails are framed as informal thank-you messages, and we will make it clear that an official tax receipt will come directly from Placer SPCA.

5. On-Site Thank-Yous:
- During event announcements, we will verbally thank sponsors and participants, and we will post a “Thank You to Our Supporters” board listing business sponsors and major donors (with their permission).

Our goal is to over-communicate gratitude so that donors feel seen, appreciated, and informed about how their contributions benefit the animals and programs at Placer SPCA.
fundraising_information.how_funds_will_be_tracked_or_reported:
We will follow a simple but structured process to ensure transparency and accuracy in how funds are collected, recorded, and reported:

1. Dedicated Event Bank Account / Sub-Account:
- I will use a separate checking account that I maintain specifically for community events and reimbursements so that event income and expenses are not mixed with my personal day-to-day finances.
- All checks will be made payable to me as the event organizer only when necessary (e.g., if a donor is unable to write a check directly to Placer SPCA), and I will promptly deposit them into this account and then issue a single consolidated check to Placer SPCA.
- Where possible, we will encourage donors to make checks payable directly to “Placer SPCA” to limit the handling of funds.

2. Online Payments and Registration Fees:
- For online registrations and pledges, we will use Eventbrite or a similar platform that provides downloadable transaction reports (including payer name, amount, and date).
- After the event, I will export a full transaction report and reconcile it against our own participant and pledge tracking spreadsheet.

3. Cash Handling at the Event:
- For cash donations and day-of registrations, we will use a clearly labeled, lockable cash box at the registration table, staffed at all times by at least two volunteers (never handled by a single person alone).
- We will use pre-numbered receipt pads for any cash donations above $20, providing a copy to the donor and keeping a copy for our records.
- At the end of the event, two volunteers (not related to each other) will count the cash together, sign off on a cash count form, and immediately deposit the cash at a nearby bank ATM or branch if possible, or secure it in a home safe until it can be deposited the next business day.

4. Expense Tracking:
- Any event-related expenses (e.g., flyer printing, park permit fees, bottled water, dog waste bags) will be tracked in a detailed spreadsheet, with copies of receipts scanned and stored in a shared Google Drive folder.
- I will clearly distinguish between donated items (in-kind donations) and items that were paid for and reimbursed from event funds.

5. Final Reconciliation and Report to Placer SPCA:
- Within two weeks of the event, I will prepare a concise final report for Placer SPCA that includes:
  - Total gross funds raised (broken down by registration fees, pledges, vendor contributions, and on-site donations).
  - Total expenses, listed by category (e.g., printing, permits, supplies).
  - Net proceeds being donated to Placer SPCA.
  - A summary of participation (number of registered walkers, approximate attendance, number of vendors, etc.).
- I will provide a copy of the final budget/ledger spreadsheet and, if requested, copies of key receipts.

6. Delivery of Funds:
- The net proceeds will be delivered to Placer SPCA as a single check, accompanied by the written report and donor list, within 14 days of the event.

This process is intended to make it easy for Placer SPCA to see exactly how funds were raised and to feel comfortable that everything was handled responsibly and transparently.
fundraising_information.whether_the_placer_spca_is_the_sole_bene:
Yes, Placer SPCA will be the sole financial beneficiary of this event. All net proceeds (after minimal, clearly documented event expenses such as park permits and printing) will be donated directly to Placer SPCA.

No other charities or organizations will receive a share of the funds raised. Local businesses participating as vendors may retain their standard sales revenue but will be encouraged to donate a portion (for example, 10–20%) of their day-of sales to Placer SPCA as well. Any such contributions will be clearly tracked and included in the final total donated to Placer SPCA.

If Placer SPCA prefers, we can also explore having registration fees or vendor donations made directly to Placer SPCA (e.g., checks written directly to Placer SPCA or use of a Placer SPCA-approved online donation link), but in all cases, Placer SPCA remains the only charitable beneficiary.
